LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS SHASHI GUPTA AND KUSHAM GUPTA MOTION TO VACATE AND SET ASIDE ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER. THE COURT STATES THAT COUNSEL MADE A GOOD FAITH EFFORT TO REACH PLAINTIFFS AND GIVE NOTICE THAT HE PLANNED TO PRESENT ARGUMENT TO THE COURT AT TODAY'S PROCEEDINGS. HEARING HELD. THE COURT HEARS ARGUMENT AND THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULES THAT THE MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. FUTURE MOTIONS MUST BE MADE WITH PROPER PROOF OF SERVICE. PLAINTIFFS MUST NOTIFY DEFENSE COUNSEL OF CURRENT AND OPERATIVE TELEPHONE NUMBER BEFORE ANY FUTURE MOTIONS ARE MADE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-18-2009 Continued To Feb-26-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Ntc And Mtn To Vacate And Set Aside The Order Sustaining Demurrer
Off Calendar Dec-03-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS KUSHAM GUPTA, and, SHASHI GUPTA Motion To Vacate and Set Aside the Order Sustaining Demurrer. Appearances: Joseph J. Siquenza, Esq. of the Law Office of Ashwani K. Bhakhri (650) 685-6334 appearing for Plaintiffs and Moving party; Jonathan H. Bornstein, Esq. of Bornstein & Bornstein (415) 409-7611, appearing for Defendant and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Defendant's counsel provided notice of intention to appear on April 29, 2009 and contest the court's tentative ruling as described in his November 2nd supplemental declaration. Plaintiffs are responsible for advising court and counsel of current and best telephone number to be reached for court related business. No excusable neglect, mistake, surprise, inadvertence or extrinsic fraud demonstrated. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Ntc And Mtn To Vacate And Set Aside The Order Sustaining Demurrer
Off Calendar Oct-07-2009 Continued To Nov-23-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS KUSHAM GUPTA, and, SHASHI GUPTA Motion To Vacate and Set Aside the Order Sustaining Demurrer. Plaintiffs Kusham Gupta and Shashi Gupta plaintiffs in Pro Per and moving party appearing; Kathryn Quetel, Esq. of Borstein & Borstein (415) 409-7611 appearing for Defendants and opposing party. Hearing held. Ruling: Matter continued to November 23, 2009 for hearing. Opposition due 11/02/09; Reply due 11/16/09. Copies of any filings should be sent directly to Dept. 302. When sending filings to opposing counsel, Plaintiffs should indicate that their filings have been signed, and that their phone number is on the upper left hand corner after their name and address. Also, the Current address in the Court's register for Plaintiffs is 3077 Alma Street, Palo Alto, CA 94306. Defense counsel to prepare Order reflecting today's ruling. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Ntc And Mtn To Vacate And Set Aside The Order Sustaining Demurrer
Off Calendar Sep-25-2009 Continued To Oct-07-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF KUSHAM GUPTA, and, SHASHI GUPTA Motion to Vacate and Set Aside the Order Sustaining Demurrer. Appearances: Plaintiffs Kusham Gupta and Shashi Gupta Plaintiffs in Pro Per and moving party appearing. Plaintiffs failed to give notice, pursuant to San Francisco Local Rule 8.3. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Continued to October 7, 2009. Defendant to respond in opposition to motion to vacate on or before October 2, 2009. Any reply due by noon, October 6, 2009. (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-31-2009 Continued To Oct-30-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Ntc And Mtn To Vacate And Set Aside Order Sustaining Demurrer
Off Calendar Jul-31-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFFS SHASHI GUPTA and KUSHAM GUPTA Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Order Sustaining Demurrer. Appearances: Jonathan Bornstein, Esq. of Bornstein & Bornstein (415) 409-7611, appearing for Defendant and opposing party. There are no other appearances. The Court states that Counsel made a Good Faith Effort to reach plaintiffs and give notice that he planned to present argument to the Court at today's proceedings. Hearing held. The Court hears argument and the Court vacates its tentative ruling and rules that the Motion is denied without prejudice. Future motions must be made with proper proof of service. Plaintiffs must notify defense counsel of current and operative telephone number before any future motions are made. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF KUSHAM GUPTA, and, SHASHI GUPTA'S Motion to Vacate and Set Aside Order Sustaining Demurrer without Leave to Amend. Appearances: Plaintiff Kusham Gupta, Plaintiff and moving party appearing in Pro Per; Kathryn Quetel, Esq. of Bornstein & Bornstein (415) 409-7611, appearing for Defendant and opposing party. The Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Off Calendar, untimely service per CCP 1005. Plaintiff may re-notice motion. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
